qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Path traversal via emitFile allows writing files outside the output directory
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Finding identifies the same vulnerable code as the advisory: Rollup's isPathFragment() in src/utils/relativeId.ts performs prefix-only validation allowing embedded ../ sequences, and writeOutputFile() in src/rollup/rollup.ts resolves paths without boundary checking. The finding demonstrates the exact same path traversal mechanism (CWE-22) described in the advisory.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unsafe deserialization in import endpoint permits attacker-controlled code execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Finding targets a different repository (example/vulnerable-repo.git) with a different vulnerability class (deserialization RCE). The advisory describes path traversal in Rollup via emitFile fileName sanitization bypass. This finding is unrelated to the advisory-described vulnerability.

selected_finding.title: Path traversal via emitFile allows writing files outside the output directory

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The path-traversal-emitfile finding matches the advisory root cause: Rollup's isPathFragment() function in src/utils/relativeId.ts only checks the first 1-2 characters of fileName, allowing embedded ../ sequences to bypass validation. The PoC successfully demonstrated arbitrary file write outside the designated output directory by calling emitFile with fileName 'subdir/../../evohunt-traversal-proof.txt', which wrote to the project root instead of dist/. This is the exact path traversal mechanism (CWE-22) described in the advisory GHSA-mw96-cpmx-2vgc.
evidence: Advisory states vulnerable code is in src/utils/sanitizeFileName.ts (INVALID_CHAR_REGEX) and src/rollup/rollup.ts (path.resolve without boundary check). Finding identifies same vulnerable logic in src/utils/relativeId.ts:14 (isPathFragment prefix-only check) and src/rollup/rollup.ts:317 (resolve without boundary validation).
PoC executed with rollup@4.58.0 (vulnerable version matching advisory affected range) successfully wrote file evohunt-traversal-proof.txt to project root instead of dist/ output directory.
Proof file contents confirm path traversal: 'This file was written outside the output directory via isPathFragment bypass in Rollup emitFile.'
Verification shows dist/ contains only input.js, while evohunt-traversal-proof.txt exists one level above in project root.
Both advisory and finding describe same attack vector: malicious plugin calling emitFile with fileName containing ../ to write outside output directory.

poc_executions.observed_effect: Rollup build completed successfully. Proof file evohunt-traversal-proof.txt was written to /Users/mac/ResearchSpace/opencode/EvoHunt-DryRun/advisory_2f67491a/.judge/poc_runs/path-traversal-emitfile/workspace/test-project/ (project root) instead of the expected dist/ output directory. The dist/ directory only contains input.js, confirming the file was written outside the designated output boundary via path traversal through the fileName 'subdir/../../evohunt-traversal-proof.txt'.
